## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about the six steps to successfully take in a webinar evergreen. So as I said, it's going to toss you about six months of optimization to get everything right, and some money to get it right. So what in the world are you doing during that period of time? Well, ideally, the process looks something like this. Don't just record a webinar, put it up and expect to succeed. There's actually six steps you go through. The first one is to build out your funnel. So you create the all the assets, the webinar, the emails, the ads, the sales page, and basically you get the whole webinar done. You run it a number of times to your warm audience and so that you build out that funnel and then, hey, celebrate this thing. Then we're going to start proving the funnel. So we've run it to our warm list. Now we're going to start running some cold traffic to it by running ads to the webinar funnel. And you're still running at this stage, the webinar live. What you're trying to do here is generate a bunch of data so that you can test and refine each element of your funnel to increase your profitability. So the target, so you know when you've finished up with this step is when you've run five consecutive webinars with at least 100 attendees in each one, not registrants attendees, each one of those webinars scoring over a 200% return on ad spend, where you're earning $2 for every $1 that you pay in advertising. So that's step two. We're taking the existing funnel that we had created for warm traffic. We're turning it into a cold traffic funnel now and we're running advertisements to it and we know we're done when we've got five consecutive webinars, 110 days each 200% return on ad spend. Next, we're now going to actually turn this into a recorded webinar. So everything else is staying the same, but we're now using a recording of your webinar. And you may be during this period of time, what you're working on doing is refining the different versions of your recording. Everything else that whole cold funnel that we set up in the last step, that's still existing. And now we're putting in different versions of the recording until we get it right. And again, our target is five consecutive webinars with 100 attendees each and 200% return on ad spend. So at this point, remember when I said everything else is staying the same.

## [03:02 - 06:02]

This is not evergreen running at every at any time during the day. These are actual scheduled webinars so that you what we're trying to do is we're trying to do this process where we're only changing one major variable at a time. If we throw in the variable times and we got two variables operating and it's very difficult to tell what's going on. So trust me, this process works one variable at a time. Step four is we switch from the scheduled webinars to variable times. So everything else stays the same. So we've got the same cold traffic funnel. We now have our recording that we've nailed down. And now we're just switching to times to people basically able to have the recorded webinar at any time that they want it to happen, including an option that starts in less than 30 minutes, which is a real key in evergreen webinars is to not just have this thing starting at four and nine p.m. It also has an option that starts in 30 minutes. Well, actually, it doesn't say within 30 minutes. It's starting at the top or the bottom of the hour. So whenever they are coming in, so if they're coming in at 45 minutes after the hour, this next webinar starts in 15 minutes at the top of the hour. Or if they're coming in at 10 minutes after the hour, the next one starts at 30 minutes after the hour. So you're just 20 minutes away from the next webinar. Your target here is to run 500 people through the variable time webinar with at least 200% return on that spent. Step five, we're going to scale our traffic. So everything else stays the same, but we're going to substantially increase the amount of traffic that we're purchasing to it. So we may have been spending $100 a day. Now we're going to start ramping that up because we're trying to get to the point where we're able to spend $500, $1,000 a day. And we're going to scale slowly about 10% in spending increase every third day, but realize by the time you watch this, the latest best practices for scaling ads may change. There are several things that I could show you right now that are the system to scale traffic right now. But that may not be true when you watch this. And so I'm wanting to do this in a way I'm just going to tell you research the best practices for scaling ads right now where you are. And your target here is ongoing 200% return on that spent. Step six is ongoing maintenance. Here you're testing every element. You're going back and retesting element every element in the funnel. So you're testing new traffic sources. So maybe you run Google ads and Instagram ads and Pinterest ads. You're starting to run different advertising mediums. You're testing new audiences.

## [06:02 - 07:41]

So you may be stretching out your audience to a wider audience. All kinds of different traffic sources that you're bringing into this can also be JV traffic. Beware of that also. You're testing each element of the funnel. So you're building out different pieces for each element of the funnel. Again, you're targeting that 200% return on ad spend. So let me just summarize this for a minute. We changed. We started off by getting it all working with warm traffic. Then we moved to cold traffic and we tested until we got consistent attendance and consistent return on ad spend. Then we started changing out individual elements. So once we've got the funnel working right, then we get the recording right. Then we got the variable times right. Then we got the skill traffic right. And then we did on ongoing maintenance. So change one variable at a time. Work with that because this is the other problem with every element. This is the other problem that everyone has. So the two problems everyone has is they slap up a webinar and expect it to work and it never does. And so then they blame it on the advertising when in reality, the problem is usually not the advertising. The problem is that their funnel is not generating sufficient trust. The second piece is that they try to change all the variables at once. And whenever you do that, you're unless you get really lucky and nail it with every element working. You usually can't make that work. So you change one key piece at a time. Those six steps walks you through it gets you to the point where you've got that amazing webinar that you've always wanted to have.
